mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals easier financial conditions and a lower discount rate for equities, so 20-day S&P 500 returns are positive when policy is in an easing phase and negative when rates are still rising.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher than their unconditional average during months when FEDFUNDS is declining, or if the sign flips and returns are negative in most easing episodes over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates above recent trend, the market reprices the path of policy higher, raising real discount rates and pressuring equities over the next month. The signal is negative only when inflation is actively surprising to the upside.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change rises above its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if upside inflation-crossing events are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the average return after triggers is not materially below the unconditional mean over any 2-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates worsening labor-market slack and weaker household income expectations, which reduces earnings expectations and compresses equity prices over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if months with rising UNRATE do not produce lower 20-day forward SPX returns than months with flat or falling UNRATE, or if the sign is positive in most late-cycle deterioration episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp slowdown in PAYEMS growth signals weakening payroll momentum before it is fully reflected in unemployment, so equities should underperform over the next month as earnings revisions turn negative.
failure_condition: Reject if negative PAYEMS momentum events are not followed by below-average 20-day SPX returns, or if more than 60% of triggered observations have positive forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS growth and HOUST growth both improve together, it signals resilient consumer spending and housing demand, which supports earnings expectations and produces positive SPX returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if joint strength in retail sales and housing starts does not outperform either series alone in explaining 20-day SPX returns, or if triggered joint-improvement windows do not show positive average returns over a 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO indicates expanding industrial production and improving real activity, which should lift cyclical earnings expectations and push S&P 500 returns higher over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO momentum does not correspond to higher 60-day forward SPX returns than neutral or declining INDPRO periods, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: After HOUST rises above its recent range, housing activity reflects improving interest-sensitive demand that feeds into construction, consumption, and risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if housing-start breakout events are not followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the average 5-day return after the trigger is not above zero over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late tightening cycle, high FEDFUNDS combined with flat or falling CPIAUCSL implies restrictive real policy that tightens financial conditions and depresses equities over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL 3-month change is falling
failure_condition: Reject if restrictive-policy episodes defined by the trigger are not followed by below-average 60-day SPX returns, or if positive returns dominate during triggered tightening windows.
